title: Exact Mertens identity via Chebyshev theta

source_refs: Erdős-647 campaign (Mnehmos/llm-driven-proof-search), Layer A quantitative-Mertens infrastructure; classical: Mertens (1874)

informal_statement: For every real x ≥ 2, the prime reciprocal sum ∑_{p≤x} 1/p equals θ(x)/(x log x) plus the weighted integral ∫_{(2,x]} (log t + 1)/(t² log² t) · θ(t) dt, where θ is Chebyshev's theta function.
informal_proof_idea: Abel summation applied to the prime indicator weighted by log p, with f(t) = 1/(t log t): the boundary term produces θ(x)/(x log x) and differentiating the weight produces the exact integral kernel (log t + 1)/(t² log² t).
formal_statement_pp:
theorem mertens_exact_theta_identity :
    ∀ x : ℝ, 2 ≤ x →
      ∑ p ∈ Finset.Icc 1 ⌊x⌋₊ with p.Prime, (1/(p:ℝ))
        = Chebyshev.theta x / (x * Real.log x)
          + ∫ t in Set.Ioc (2:ℝ) x,
              (Real.log t + 1) / (t^2 * (Real.log t)^2) * Chebyshev.theta t

review.review_basis: repo self-review; kernel-verified through the tracked proof-search loop

notes: Neutral Prime Distribution port (mathcorpus #12 / proofsearch #253) of Erdős-647 campaign theorem erdos647_mertens_identity (problem_version d584666d-e50d-488d-b459-5d1265a3aadd). Statement and proof are the kernel-verified campaign snapshot verbatim modulo the neutral rename; proof authority is the Lean kernel via the tracked proof-search loop.
